feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition – All Communication Scenarios in One Place (Bookmark)
entry.content:
Make sure to bookmark this blog so you can easily revisit all communication scenarios in one place. Share it with your friends and colleagues—it’s a handy resource when searching across different SAP portals.

What is a Communication Scenario ?

A Communication Scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is a predefined, packaged set of:

  • Inbound/Outbound APIs (OData, SOAP)
  • Business events (for event-based integration)
  • Message types
  • Authorizations

These scenarios act as the integration between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and other SAP Products or external systems.

Think of a communication scenario as a “bundle” :

These are the APIs and permissions this external system is allowed to use, and here’s how the communication should happen.

Each Communication Scenario is delivered by SAP S/4HANA Public Product Release and cannot be modified—but you can activate and use them.

Key Components of a Communication Scenario

A typical scenario includes:

1. Communication User

A technical user used for authentication.
Supports:

  • Basic Authentication
  • OAuth 2.0 (strongly recommended)

2. Communication System

Represents the external (non‑SAP) or SAP system you are integrating with.
Defines:

  • Hostname / Token URLs
  • Outbound target URLs
  • Authentication method

3. Communication Arrangement

This is where everything comes together.

You assign:

  • The Communication Scenario
  • The Communication System
  • The Communication User
  • Any specific parameters (e.g., service URLs, outbound destinations)

If you think of it simply:

Scenario = Template
Arrangement = Active Configuration

Why Are Communication Scenarios Important?

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, customers cannot create custom communication scenarios like in on‑premise or Private Cloud. This strict governance ensures:

  • Security and compliance
  • System stability
  • Standardized integration patterns
  • Ease of lifecycle management during upgrades

So instead of customizing scenarios, you configure and connect the right SAP‑delivered scenarios.

APIs usually come with a predefined communication scenario. To use the APIs, you first need to create a communication arrangement based on the communication scenario.

How to Configure a Communication Scenario: Step-by-Step

Step 1: Create a Communication User

SAP Fiori App: Maintain Communication Users

  • Create user → assign password → store securely
  • Or create an OAuth technical user (preferred)

Step 2: Create a Communication System

SAP Fiori App: Communication Systems

Here you:

  • Assign the communication user
  • Maintain authentication method
  • Provide target host details
  • Configure OAuth token endpoint (if applicable)

Step 3: Create Communication Arrangement

SAP Fiori App: Communication Arrangements

Steps:

  1. Select the communication scenario (e.g., SAP_COM_0008)
  2. Assign the communication system
  3. Review/activate inbound and outbound services
  4. Copy Service URLs for external system use

Once activated, the APIs are ready for use.

Testing Your Scenario

You can test APIs using tools like:

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub (API Hub)
  • SAP Integration Suite (CPI)
  • SAP API Management
  • SAP BTP  (Destinations, Side by Side Extensions, Extensibility Development)
  • Custom integrations

Check which communication scenario fits to your purposes best.

The following list gives you an overview of the most important communication scenarios available today

entry.title: Gregor Wolf
entry.content:
About Gregor

  • Germany | SAP Mentor since 2007 | Follow Gregor on LinkedIn

I am an independent SAP solution architect, developer and SAP Mentor. I focus on SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), Node.JS, SAP Fiori Elements and Security. I've supported several customers during their SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition (S/4HANA On Premise) implementation. Since the end of 2024 I support the technical topics at an SAP Cloud ERP Public Edition Customer.

Topics of interest: SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), SAP Cloud ERP public and private, SAPUI5, OpenUI5, SAP Fiori, SAP HANA Cloud

Gregor, what inspired you to become an SAP Mentor?

A key influence early in my SAP Community journey was Mark Finnern. He is the father of the SAP Community. Mark encouraged me to move beyond answering questions and start sharing detailed technical how-to's but also event reports in blog posts. In 2007 Mark also founded the SAP Mentors program which gave me the opportunity to provide structured, experience-based feedback to SAP product teams and help bridge the gap between product vision and real-world implementation.

What advice would like to share with other SAP community members?

My advice to other SAP Community members is simple: contribute consistently and think long-term.

Start by answering questions and helping others solve real problems - but don’t stop there. Document your lessons learned, share architectural insights, and write about what worked and what didn’t. That’s how collective expertise grows. And you will be amazed how often you research years later and find your own answers and blog posts to solve a problem you're just facing. Despite all the new stuff SAP has quite a long tail that doesn't change quickly.

Engage constructively, challenge respectfully, and focus on signal over noise. The community is not just a support forum - it’s an ecosystem where practitioners can influence product direction, elevate standards, and shape the future of SAP together.

entry.title: Communication Arrangements in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Communication Arrangements

Communication Arrangements is one of the Key User Extensibility Tools.

With Communication Arrangements App,

  • Communication Arrangements details can be viewed.
  • Custom Communication Arrangements can be created with standard communication or custom communication scenarios using Communication Arrangements App.

Finding the Standard Communication Scenarios in SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Navigate to SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Choose the Product

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition

Navigate to APIs -> ODATA V2 / ODATA V4.

Search Required API

List of results for Purchase Order.

Purchase Order API Overview

API Resources

Navigate to API Resources -> Attributes -> Communication Scenario

API Specifications

Authentication Methods for API

Configuration Details for API

Extensibility

Custom Fields can be added to the API using below Business Contexts - Header and Items in Custom Fields App with Key User Extensibility.

API Reference

List of Entity Sets Available in the APIs.

Communication Scenarios

Navigate to Display Communication Scenarios,

All these are Standard Communication Scenarios.

Standard Communication Scenarios of Purchase Order - SAP_COM_0053

Steps to Create Communication Arrangements

Maintain Communication Users

Navigate to Maintain Communication Users App.

Maintain Communication Users is to create Communication Users for the API Access.

Choose New button to create Communication User

Add User Name and Password.

Propose Password can be chosen to generate password by system.

Save the Communication User.

If communication User is added to any Communication system or communication Arrangements, here it shows those details.

Communication System

Navigate to Communication System App.

Communication System is to maintain communication system with host name and communication User.

Provide Communication System ID and Name.

Add the Host Name

Add necessary details

Choose Add button to add Inbound Communication User.

Available Authentication Methods

Required details for SSL Client Certificate.

Required details for OAuth 2.0

Choose the Authentication Method, and choose User Name/Client ID.

Click on Ok after choosing Communication User.

Communication User is added.

 Communication System is saved.

Communication Arrangements

Navigate to Communication Arrangements app.

Communication Arrangements is to generate Inbound Service URLs by adding Communication System and Communication User.

Choose New Option to create Custom Communication Arrangements.

Standard or Custom Communication Scenarios is required to create Custom Communication Arrangements.

Search for the Standard or Custom Communication Scenario

‘SAP_COM_0053’ is the Standard Communication Scenario for the Purchase Order API.

Arrangement appears automatically based on Communication Scenario and it can be changed.

Click on Create button to create Communication Arrangement.

List of Inbound Services available for that Communication Scenario.

WSDL/Service Metadata will be generated once Saved the Communication Arrangement.

Communication System and Communication User is required to create Communication Arrangements.

WSDL/Service Metadata is generated.

Service Metadata

Service URL is available in Communication Arrangement.

Access the Service URLs through the Communication User details.

Conclusion

standard communication or custom communication scenarios are used to create Communication Arrangements.

In Communication Arrangements, OData URLs are generated based on inbound Service.

References

  • Communication Arrangements | SAP Help Portal
  • Maintain Communication Users | SAP Help Portal
  • Communication Systems | SAP Help Portal
  • Integration | SAP Help Portal
  • Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: "Getting Started: Maximizing Your Potential from the Start"
entry.content:
Overview of the three main content areas and how they support your development.

  1. Discussions – Learn Together
    Connect with peers to ask questions, share knowledge, and collaborate on learning. Our experts are available to provide guidance and answers if needed.
  2. Blog – Stay Informed and Inspired
    Stay informed on product developments, features, and certification paths with regular updates to enhance your skills.
  3. Knowledge Base – Official Learning Updates
    Central source for official announcements related to learning content, including new or updated Learning Journeys, certification changes, new course releases, and invitations to live sessions or expert-led trainings.

Tour the SAP Community - Explore the various features, functionality, and aspects of the SAP Community, familiarize yourself with community guidelines, and discover how to get the most out of your membership.

START Learning based on your proficiency level

  1. Start with the Basics
    Positioning SAP Business Suite     - Begin with a general overview to understand the positioning and value of SAP Business Suite. This will give you a solid foundation before moving into specific topics.
  2. Choose Your Line of Business
    Next, focus on the area that interests you most    . This helps you understand the solutions and processes within your chosen domain. For example:
    • Positioning SAP Financial Management Solutions
    • Positioning SAP Supply Chain Management Solutions
  3. Deepen Your Knowledge
    Continue with a specific Line of Business and deepen your expertise. For example:
    • Positioning Enterprise Performance Management
    • Positioning Financial Planning & Analysis
    • Positioning Accounting & Financial Close
    • Positioning the SAP Business Suite for Governance, Risk and Compliance
  4. Become an Expert
    If you want to move towards implementation, explore product-focused learning     offering such as:
    • SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • SAP Certified Specialist - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Recommended Communities

We invite you to become a member and subscribe to the communities below for in-depth discussions on Finance and Supply Chain Management::

  • Welcome to our Financial Management Learning Group - SAP Community
  • Welcome to our Supply Chain Management Learning Gr... - SAP Community

Happy Learning 😊

entry.title: Resolving Key Mapping Missing Issues while sending Freight Unit from Cloud ERP to On Premise system
entry.content:
Introduction

The process for Scope Item 6W1 for External Transportation Planning enables transportation requests created in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP to be planned within an SAP On Premise system. In this case, Freight units generated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP can be transferred to the On Premise system (with SAP TM) for detailed transportation planning.

Once planning is completed, the results are sent back and integrated into SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP in the form of freight orders. Upon transfer, transportation charges for these freight orders are calculated automatically. Based on the finalized freight orders, outbound deliveries can then be initiated.

This post covers the scenario where an error is received and the possible workaround for the error.

Kindly note that below steps are a workaround and steps must be taken to resolve error by undertaking the master data replication as per the set up guide.

Process Details

Complete the set up for the scenario including the set up of communication scenario and Data replication. The process requires the Purchase order be created within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP. Ensure that the Purchase group is selected as "006" External TM Planning (refer below screenshot).

Based on settings a Transportation Management Freight unit is created. The same can be seen within the Purchase order app under the tab "TM Status"

As the Process needs the Freight Unit planning within the external TM system the scope items 6W1 in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP should be activated. Once done the Freight Unit from Public Cloud system is replicated to the On Premise system.

This replication message should create a Freight Unit within the On Premise / Decentralized TM system.

Challenge

In case of Failure of the FU replication, check the message within the SAP Message Monitor to identify the type of error. One of the error that can occur may relate to Invalid data. As you can see in the below screenshot the error related to invalid data.

This blog covers on the process to be followed to resolve this issue.

To resolve the issue, as you can see the below warnings need to be removed to resolve the error. These warning relate to incorrect / missing key mapping for different Master Data Objects like Location / Business Partner / Product between the Public Cloud and the On Premise system. This can happen in case the replication was not performed correctly.

Resolution

To resolve the issue, key mapping needs to be maintained in both the Public Cloud and the On Premise system.

You can follow the following steps for the same.

  1. Use the Tcode MDG_KM_MAINTAIN within the On Premise SAP system. Manage Key Mapping UI opens.

  1. For each of the impacted Business Object Type (as per the error) select the Object Type (example Business Partner in above screenshot) and Object Type ID, Business System and the Master Data ID (example Business partner ID).
  2. Click on Add Row to add the entries as below. Fill in the details such that there are 2 rows each of Public cloud and On Premise system
    1. Row 1 with Public Cloud System ID and BP number and Row 2 for BP UUID in the standard format: 8-4-4-4-12 (hexadecimal groupings). Example – If location UUID is 355F4B6A67ED1EEE9297A3E7521BF064 it should be mentioned as 355F4B6A-67ED-1EEE-9297-A3E7521BF064
    2. Repeat the same for the On Premise system with corresponding details added in row 3 & 4.

 

  1. Save
  2. Repeat the same process from steps 1 to 4 for all other Master data objects as per the error.
  3. Repeat the same process from steps 1 to 5 within the Public Cloud system using the app „Manage Key Mapping“.

Rerun the process of FU replication and mapping error should be resolved now.

entry.title: Automated Invoice and Credit Memo Processing Using ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Automated Invoice and Credit Memo Processing Using ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Introduction

In a standard procurement process, materials are procured from a supplier through purchase order creation, goods receipt posting, invoice processing, and vendor payment. When Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS) is activated, the system automatically creates the supplier invoice based on the goods receipt, eliminating the need for manual invoice posting.

During one of the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementations, a requirement came up to handle supplier return scenarios using ERS. In cases where materials were found defective after receipt and payment, the business expected the system to automatically generate a credit memo once the returned goods were posted. This blog explains the end-to-end process that was configured and tested in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to achieve this automation.

Step 1: Maintain Business Partner Master Data

 Use the Manage Business Partner Master Data app.

Ensure the following fields are enabled for the Supplier in the Purchasing tab:

  • Automatic Evaluated Receipt Settlement
  • Evaluated Receipt Settlement
  • Goods Receipt-Based Invoice Verification

These settings allow the system to generate invoices and credit memos automatically without manual invoice posting.

Step 1: Image 1

Step 2: Schedule ERS Background Job 

Use the app: Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs – Advanced

Step 2: Image 1

Select the job template: Evaluated Receipt Settlement

This job automatically:

  • Creates Supplier Invoices for GR
  • Creates Credit Memos for Returns

I have created scheduled background jobs for ERS using this app.

Step 2: Image 2

 

Step 2: Image 3

Step 2: Image 4

Step 2: Image 5

Step 3: Create Standard Purchase Order (ERS Enabled)

Create a standard PO.

Ensure:

 ERS checkbox is automatically enabled in the Invoice tab.

Step 3: Image 1

Post Goods Receipt (GRN).

Once the scheduled ERS job runs: System automatically generates the Supplier Invoice document. The below image shows the PO history reflecting the automatically created invoice document.

Step 3: Image 2

In the Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs – Advanced app, the below image shows the scheduled job that automatically generated the supplier invoice based on the ERS configuration.

Step 3: Image 3

Step 3: Image 4

Note: In SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition), the classic GUI transaction MRRL used for Evaluated Receipt Settlement is not available. Instead, ERS processing is executed through the Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs - Advanced app.

Step 4: Vendor Payment

Based on the automatically generated invoice:

  • Vendor payment is processed and cleared using standard payment process.

Return Process Using ERS

Step 5: Create Return Purchase Order

When defective material needs to be returned:

  • Create PO with Return Indicator
  • Ensure ERS is enabled in the PO

Step 5: Image 1

Step 6: Post Return Goods Receipt

Post Goods Receipt for the Return PO.

Once the scheduled ERS job runs:

  • System automatically generates a Credit Memo
  • No manual invoice posting required
  • Liability is adjusted automatically

Step 6: Image 1

In the Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs – Advanced app, the below image shows the scheduled job that automatically generated the credit memo for the supplier return process based on the ERS configuration.

Step 6: Image 2

Step 6: Image 3

Conclusion

In my experience, ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud works smoothly not only for regular procurement but also for supplier return scenarios, provided the configuration is done correctly. Once the supplier master is properly maintained and the background job is scheduled, the system automatically takes care of invoice generation for goods receipts and credit memo creation for returns.

The key is to ensure that ERS is consistently enabled in both the standard and return purchase orders. When everything is aligned, the process runs without manual intervention, reducing effort for the finance team and avoiding posting errors. This makes the overall P2P cycle more controlled and efficient.

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud: How to Retrieve Print Queue PDFs via OData API
entry.content:
Introduction

When building side-by-side extensions or integrating external applications with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, a common requirement is to retrieve generated PDF documents (like Invoices or Purchase Orders) programmatically.SAP provides a dedicated standard pattern: The Print Queue API (SAP_COM_0466).
Overview | Print Queue Item - Read (A2X) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Step 1: Communication Setup

First, you need to expose the API through a Communication Arrangement.

  1. Create a Communication User: Set up a technical user and password in the Maintain Communication Users app.

     

    ex. SHU_TEST_UESR

     

  2. Activate the Arrangement: Create a new arrangement for scenario SAP_COM_0466 (Printing - Pull Integration).

     


    Important: Ensure the arrangement is saved and its status is Active. API permissions will not trigger if it remains in "Draft" mode.

Step 2: Print Queue Configuration (The Secret Sauce)

This is where most developers get stuck. Even with an active API, the service will return empty results unless the Print Queue is explicitly configured to allow your technical user.

  1. Open the Maintain Print Queues app.
  2. Create a New Queue: Do not use the DEFAULT queue, as it often has restricted permissions. Create a custom one (e.g., Z_PULL_TEST).
  3. Assign the User: Inside the queue settings, navigate to the Assigned Users section and add your Communication User.
Without this assignment, the API will return a 200 OK but with zero data, as the user doesn't have "permission to see" the contents of that specific vault.

Step 3: Retrieving the PDF via API

The service API_CLOUD_PRINT_PULL_SRV uses Function Imports. You must follow a specific sequence of calls using Basic Authentication.

1. Verify Connectivity

Check which queues your user can access:

GET
.../sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CLOUD_PRINT_PULL_SRV/Get_PrintQueuesOfUser

2. Fetch the Item ID

Retrieve the ID for the next available document in the queue:

GET
.../RetrieveNextQueueItem?Qname='Z_EXTERNAL_PULL'&Language='EN'

Copy the item_id from the XML response (e.g., FA163ECE...).

 

3. Download the PDF Content

Use the Item ID to pull the Base64 encoded binary data:

GET
.../Get_QItem_Document?attachments=false&main_doc=true&item_id='YOUR_ITEM_ID'&qname='Z_EXTERNAL_PULL'

Step 4: Handling the Result

The response will contain a <d:Blob> tag. This is a Base64 encoded string of the PDF. To view the content, you simply need to decode this string in your application logic (or use a tool like Postman’s Visualizer).

 

<d:Blob>JVBERi0xLj... (Long Base64 String)</d:Blob>
Pro Tip: Once downloaded, it is best practice to call the Set_QItem_Status function to mark the document as "Printed" or "Successful" so it is removed from the pending pull list.

Conclusion

By shifting from the internal Output Management service to the Print Queue Pull API (SAP_COM_0466), you can reliably and securely automate document retrieval from S/4HANA Cloud. The key takeaways are ensuring the Active status of your Communication Arrangement and the explicit Assignment of the User to the custom Print Queue.

Happy coding!
Shusei Sekiya

entry.title: Workflow for Sales quotations and Sales Orders triggered when the end user changes the Item Price
entry.content:
This requirement is to be implemented in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

To trigger a workflow in the Sales Quotation and Sales Order applications, a condition must be checked for the item price. The workflow should be triggered only if the item price has been changed by the end user. To achieve this, a sales quotation or sales order simulation needs to be executed to determine the system-calculated item price. This value must then be compared with the price entered or modified by the user. If a difference is identified, the workflow should be triggered accordingly.

The main challenge is retrieving the item price within the custom logic. However, this can be addressed by calling the Sales Order Simulation API, which provides the calculated item price for comparison.

Pre-requisties : 

  • Required Business Roles must be assigned to the user. 
  • Workflows for Sales Orders must be configured and activated in the system. Refer SAP Help on flexible workflow setup.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/5a5f3f686c06490893a588c99b36adca.html

Solution: 

When sales quotation and sales order created/changed in S/4 system then the Business Add-In (BAdI) SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON is provided to specify whether a sales document is relevant for approvals or not. Refer below documentation for this BADI.  https://api.sap.com/badi/SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON

In this Blog, Sales order is used for reference to explain the use case. 

Communication Setup:

  1. In order to execute API for simulation of the creation of sales order inside Cloud Badi implementation (SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON),  required communcation arrangement to be setup for the Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0109 (Sales Order Integration) to technically enable and activate the Sales Order simulation API - 'API_SALESORDERSIMULATION'.  Refer API Hub for detail :  https://api.sap.com/api/sap-s4-CE_SALESORDERSIMULATION_0001-v1/overview
  2. Refer SAP Help for guidance on setting up the communication arrangement, system and communication user.   https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/2e84a10c430645a88bdbfaaa23ac9ff7.html
  3. After setup of communication arrangement, we will have the Sales Order Simulation API endpoint to be used inside Custom BADI.
  4.  Since, the Sales order simulation API to be called within the S/4 system itself, so custom communcation scenario to be setup to enable the call of inbound APIs inside Cloud Badi.  In custom communcation scenario, generic outbound service for REST type to be added. Communication arrangement to be created for this custom communication scenario to activate HTTP service with same outbound user as was used in step 1(setup of standard communcation scenrio).  Refer SAP Help document for additional details.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/8d5d71252edd449982083cb5a3b8c8c4-182.html
  5. After completing the above steps, custom communication scenario, outbound service and Sales order simulation API is ready to be used inside implementaton of cloud BADI SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON."

Cloud BADI implementation

  1. Create Implementation of Cloud BADI 'SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON' via Custom Logic app (Key User Extensibility).
  2. Add required checks based on importing parameters - SALESDOCUMENT(Sales Document for Approvals)  and  SALESDOCUMENTITEM (Sales Document Item Table for Approvals). For instance : type of order, sales origanzation, sales document categoty.
  3. Configuration check of communication arrangment, service activation and API Call request-response. 
  • Check if outbound service exists in the communication scenario.

                      CHECK cl_ble_http_client=>is_service_available(
                             communication_scenario = 'YY1_COMM_SCENARIO'
                              outbound_service = 'YY1_COMM_OUTBOUND_REST'
                                    ) = abap_true.

  •         Creation of the HTTP client to access the outbound service
               DATA(lo_client) = cl_ble_http_client=>create(
               communication_scenario = 'YY1_COMM_SCENARIO'
              outbound_service = 'YY1_COMM_OUTBOUND_REST'
                 ).
  •          Creation of service request and set properties  
             *method used for service call
       lo_request = cl_ble_http_request=>create( ).                                                                            
       lo_request->set_header_parameter( EXPORTING name = 'x-csrf-token' value = lv_csrf ).
       lo_request->set_content_type( 'application/json' ).

             * Post method to execute Sales Order Simulation API. URL here is endpoint ('/api_salesordersimulation/srvd_a2x/sap/salesordersimulation/0001/SalesOrder' )
           lo_request->set_method( 'POST' )->set_resource_extension( lv_url ).

* Payload in JSON to be created for passing Sales order header data from SALESDOCUMENT and item data from SALESDOCUMENTITEM.

           Sample Payload :                     

{
  "SalesOrderType": "TA",
  "SalesOrganization": "XXXX",
  "DistributionChannel": "10",
  "OrganizationDivision": "10",
  "SoldToParty": "XXXXXXXXX",
  "_Item": [
    {
       "Product": "XXXX",
      "RequestedQuantity": 1,
      "RequestedQuantityISOUnit" : "PCE"
    },
        {    
      "Product": "XXXX",
      "RequestedQuantity": 2,
      "RequestedQuantityISOUnit" : "PCE"}
  ]  }
  • Call the request by passing Payload created and get the response. 
        lo_request->set_body( lv_payload ).
    * ***Send API request
    lo_response = lo_client->send( lo_request ).
    ***Getting response body from response received
    DATA(lv_body) = lo_response->get_body( ).                                                                                                  
  • From the API response, the value of 'TotalNetAmount' needs to be retrieved.
    The standard class method /ui2/cl_json=>deserialize should be used by passing the response body as input and extracting the value of 'TotalNetAmount' from the resulting structure.

Note: The custom wrapper class is created in ADT(Developer extensbility) to be called inside Cloud logic. This customer class used standard method /ui2/cl_json->deserialize_json to extract the attribute value of  'TotalNetAmount'.

/ui2/cl_json->deserialize_json(EXPORTING
im_payload = lv_body
IMPORTING
ex_total_val = DATA(lv_field_amt) ).

  • Compare the TotalNetAmount entered by the user during Sales Order creation (importing parameter: salesdocument-totalnetamount) with the Total Net Amount retrieved from the API response.  If there is a difference, set the changing parameter SALESDOCAPPROVALREASON  accordingly.

Conclusion : Additional specific complex conditions can be determined inside custom logic to check if Sales order workflow to be triggered or not. For instance, POST operation(simlulation mode) for released API's  can be called to validate conditions inside Custom Logic(Key user extensibility) in S/4 HANA Public Cloud System.

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1
entry.content:
Preliminary version of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1.

You can find this preview on the current SAP Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

For any feedback you may have, try the Commenting Feature for What's New documentation in this release. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Learning Map
entry.content:
The ABAP Cloud development model (ABAP Cloud) is the foundation of ABAP-based development at SAP, its partners, and customers, designed from the ground up to adhere to clean core principles. It brings together evolving technologies and methodologies into a sustainable, long-lasting programming model. 

The ABAP Cloud development model encompasses all aspects of modern cloud-ready software development, resulting in a wide range of topics that need to be understood. Therefore, clear guidance is helpful for getting started with and navigating ABAP Cloud.

Discover the ABAP Cloud Learning Map🗺️ – your guide to mastering the ABAP Cloud development model. It provides an overview of essential topics and skills, along with a curated collection of links to key resources covering the technologies, tools, approaches, and practices that drive cloud-ready, upgrade-stable ABAP development. 📚Whether you’re starting out or filling knowledge gaps, the learning map helps you navigate modern ABAP Cloud development at your own pace. 

🔗Access it here: ABAP Cloud Learning Map↗

entry.title: Where ORD Is Used in SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud & SAP Databricks
entry.content:
SAP Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is becoming a foundational standard across the SAP ecosystem because it provides a consistent, self‑describing metadata layer for APIs, events, business objects, and data products. This metadata standardization is what enables smooth interoperability across SAP’s modern data and integration platforms.

Below are the key places where ORD is used today — and how it empowers seamless integration, especially with SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud, and SAP Databricks.

1. SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com)

SAP Business Accelerator Hub uses ORD as one of the primary mechanisms for publishing APIs and events. Any API showing an ORD ID on its overview page is officially ORD-enabled, meaning it exposes standardized metadata that can be consumed by SAP tools and data platforms.

This ensures:

  • Consistent API descriptions
  • Discoverability by SAP’s central aggregators
  • Compatibility with downstream systems like SAP Business Data Cloud
This is the first indicator that an API can participate in the broader data product ecosystem. 
 

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

SAP uses ORD internally to improve SAP-to-SAP integration and provide a unified metadata layer that development tools (like SAP Business Application Studio) can consume for enhanced API discovery and automation.

Each Product Owner from SAP Products LoB will be develop, design ORD to make it available in SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com) and Data Products Marketplace for you..

3. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)

SAP Business Data Cloud actively uses ORD to describe Data Products and make them discoverable to consumers across the SAP ecosystem, including SAP Datasphere. ORD helps BDC deliver a unified catalog with:

  • Descriptions of datasets
  • Relationships
  • Capabilities
  • Higher-level metadata structures like data products and business entities
In addition, third‑party systems such as SAP Databricks are required to describe data products using ORD before publishing them into SAP Business Data Cloud.
 
This ensures smooth catalog integration and consistent metadata across SAP and non-SAP data sources.
 

4. SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere consumes data products published to SAP Business Data Cloud — and because BDC requires ORD‑based metadata, Datasphere gains:

  • Automatically discoverable data products
  • Standardized metadata for modeling
  • Compatibility with external data such as Databricks Delta Lake

Datasphere uses ORD indirectly because it relies on BDC’s ORD-driven catalog for understanding and federating data assets.

Additionally, SAP’s hybrid integration patterns show that Datasphere can integrate with Databricks using JDBC or federated models — and ORD ensures data products between these systems are consistently described.

5. SAP Databricks (Enterprise Databricks Integration)

SAP Databricks integrates deeply with SAP Business Data Cloud using BDC Connect, enabling bi-directional sharing of data products. To publish a data product from SAP Databricks to SAP Business Data Cloud:

  • The metadata must be described using ORD and CSN (Core Schema Notation)
  • The data product becomes discoverable to SAP systems (e.g., Datasphere) via BDC

Furthermore, Databricks–SAP integration uses Delta Share, JDBC, and federated models, but ORD plays the critical role of standardizing data product metadata.

This creates a smooth, unified integration path:

SAP applications → ORD-based metadata → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere & Databricks

SAP Architecture Center also confirms this hybrid pattern where business data from SAP and non‑SAP (Databricks) is harmonized through Business Data Cloud and Datasphere, enabling interoperable AI/ML and analytics scenarios.

 Refer my SAP blog

6. Data Products Across the SAP Ecosystem

ORD also provides standardized metadata for:

  • Entity Types / Business Objects
  • APIs and Events
  • Data Products 
This consistency is the key enabler for cross‑platform interoperability — especially as Data Products become central to the SAP ecosystem via Business Data Cloud and Datasphere.

Summary: How ORD Enables Smooth Integration Across SAP & Databricks

With ORD in place:

  • APIs with an ORD ID on api.sap.com can be safely consumed to build data products in SAP Business Data Cloud.
  • Business Data Cloud uses ORD metadata to catalog and serve data products to SAP Datasphere and SAP Databricks.
  • SAP Databricks uses ORD when publishing its own data products back into Business Data Cloud.
  • SAP Datasphere leverages ORD-driven metadata to discover, model, and federate data products seamlessly.

ORD is the metadata “glue” that ensures smooth integration across:

SAP BTP → Business Accelerator Hub → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere → Databricks

And the more SAP and partner solutions adopt ORD, the smoother, more automated, and more scalable integrations become across the SAP landscape.

entry.title: Maintain Form Templates in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Maintain Form Templates can be used to create Custom Form Templates using standard templates in SAP S.4 HANA Public Cloud with Key User Extensibility.

Maintain Form Templates is a tile to create new Custom Form Templates in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud.

 

Maintain Form Templates in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud

  • Maintain Form Templates App views a list of all available form templates and filter the templates by their assigned languages or countries
  • With this, create a new custom form template with one original language and the option to translate it to different target languages
  • Copy an SAP-predelivered template to custom form templates for the next editing and reuse
  • Delete custom form templates that are no longer needed
  • Access older versions of the custom template, download them and compare two versions at object level
  • Custom fields can also be added to the Custom form templates and values can be mapped using custom logic.

Navigate to Maintain Form Templates App

Predelivered Templates

Choose filters to filter the templates with required templates.

This is the Predelivered template of Purchase Order

Choose Download to edit this template in Adobe Life Cycle Designer

These are the files of the form template.

Custom Templates

For the development of custom form templates, the form template file (XDP) can be downloaded to edit it with the Adobe LiveCycle Designer and upload it as custom template in the original language.

Choose New to create custom template.

Provide the Custom Template name, choose the downloaded file as template file,

choose Dats Source same as Purchase Order template Data Source and

Form Type as Content.

Custom form Template

Save the Custom Form Template

Download the Custom Form Template file to make the necessary changes and re-upload it.

Downloaded files of the Custom Form template.

Adobe LifeCycle Designer for SAP Solutions

Open Adobe LifeCycle Designer to edit the custom form

Choose the form

This is the standard form template, and this can be changeable.

Re upload the custom form after making necessary changes.

Choose Custom Form Template

Custom Form Template has been uploaded.

Assign Form Templates

Assign the custom form templates

Navigate to Assign Form Templates

Maintain an entry for the Custom Form Templates

Maintain the entry for the custom Form Templates as same as standard templates.

Choose new entries.

Custom form template has been maintained.

Output Parameter Determination

Maintain custom form template in output parameter determination

Navigate to Output Parameter Determination

Choose Purchase Order

Choose Form Template as determination

Add a entry or edit the existing entries for custom form templates.

Choose Output type as Purchase Order

Choose Channel as Print

Choose Sender as IN

Add custom form template name in form template

Check and activate the changes.

Testing Form Templates

Navigate to create purchase order and check the forms generated.

Custom form template is triggered.

This is the custom form template

Conclusion

In SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud, Custom Form templates with specific document types can be created using standard form templates in Maintain form Template app.

Those Custom Form template can be download and modify the form design in Adobe Life Cycle designer and same can be uploaded to that form.

These Custom Form Template can be configured in Output Parameter determination for respective business process.

References

  • Maintain Form Templates | SAP Help Portal
  • Output Management | SAP Help Portal
  • Output Parameter Determination | SAP Help Portal
  • Assign Form Template to Output Type | SAP Help Portal
  • Assign Form Templates | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: On-Stack Partner Reference App: A Guide to Clean-Core Extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
As SAP partners and customers continue to innovate in the SAP cloud ERP space, one principle remains non-negotiable: keep the core clean. While SAP’s extensibility guidelines often favor side-by-side builds on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), some scenarios require tighter, real-time integration with core processes. For these tightly coupled cases, on-stack extensibility within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides the essential solution. It offers a powerful way to enhance standard business processes—without modifying SAP-delivered code—enabling end users to innovate securely and at scale.

To bridge theory and practice, the On-Stack Partner Reference Application provides a practical blueprint for building these clean-core extensions. Using modern ABAP and RAP principles, it demonstrates how to extend standard SAP applications in a secure, upgrade-stable, and scalable way, fully aligned with SAP guidelines.

 When to Choose On-Stack Extensibility

While a side-by-side architecture on SAP Business Technology Platform is a powerful approach, it isn’t the ideal fit for every business need. Sometimes, an extension must be tightly woven into the main system to deliver the best results. On-stack extensibility is the recommended path for these tightly coupled scenarios, especially when your solution requires:

  • Deep integration with standard business processes
  • Seamless navigation across SAP Fiori applications
  • A harmonized user experience
  • Enhancements that are upgrade-safe within the same system

On-stack extensibility allows developers and organizations to achieve these goals by building directly within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud stack. This approach preserves the clean-core architecture, ensuring the system remains stable and maintainable. The Partner Reference Application demonstrates the recommended “golden path” for achieving this perfect balance.

The Reference Scenario: Loyalty Hub

When on-stack extensibility is the right approach, the On-Stack Partner Reference Application provides a hands-on example that demonstrates how to extend standard SAP processes securely and efficiently. It goes beyond theory to present a full end-to-end scenario that partners can explore, adapt, and use as a practical blueprint.

The scenario uses a Loyalty Hub application integrated with the standard Manage Sales Orders – Version 2 SAP Fiori application. Its goal is to help organizations manage customer loyalty programs directly within sales operations, while preserving seamless navigation between standard SAP functionality and the custom extension.

To make the scenario concrete, we demonstrate the application using two personas:

  • Aarti, the loyalty administrator, manages memberships, awards loyalty points, and issues gift cards
  • Julia, the sold-to party or customer, earns points, redeems rewards, and applies gift cards during sales transactions

This approach highlights both administrative and customer perspectives, showing how Loyalty Hub supports real-world workflows and interactions. Here’s how the Loyalty Hub works from both the administrative and customer perspectives:

Key Capabilities

The application delivers comprehensive loyalty management:

  • Handles memberships, tiers, and related transactions
  • Tracks and awards loyalty points automatically in response to sales order events
  • Issues and redeems gift cards tied to customer orders
  • Sends automated notifications for points updates and tier changes
  • Supports both administrator workflows and end-user interactions

Technical and Operational Design

  • Built with ABAP RAP and custom business objects in the Z namespace
  • Data modeled using CDS, ensuring separation from standard SAP objects and upgrade stability
  • User interfaces designed with SAP Fiori elements, supporting flexible extensions and semantic navigation
  • SAP-standard authentication and role-based authorization for secure access
  • Operational readiness includes scheduled jobs for membership evaluation, number range handling for controlled object creation, and application logging/messaging for monitoring and troubleshooting

This scenario illustrates how partners can innovate within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud stack, extending functionality while preserving clean-core principles, upgrade safety, and a consistent SAP user experience — all while reflecting both administrative and customer perspectives.

Key Business Value

The reference application helps your organization accelerate development while staying fully compliant with clean-core principles. By using it as a guide, your teams can:

  • Gain a reference architecture aligned with SAP standards.
  • Use proven patterns to integrate with standard applications.
  • Learn to build reusable and deployable solutions.
  • Accelerate the delivery of robust and upgrade-safe extensions.

The Loyalty Hub demonstrates that innovation and compliance can coexist—adding real business value without compromising the SAP clean core. The best way to understand the power of this approach is to see it in action.

You can get started and try the Loyalty Hub example by accessing the complete reference application on GitHub. By following this framework, you can confidently deliver the innovative, compliant, and upgrade-safe solutions your business requires.

On Stack Partner Reference Extension for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Explore Other Reference Applications

Depending on your extensibility strategy, you can also explore:

  • Partner Reference Application for SAP BTP ABAP , Blog
  • Cross-Stack Partner Reference Extension
  • Partner Reference Application for SAP BTP and CAP

Together, these resources guide developers and architects across on-stack, side-by-side, and cross-stack extension patterns—helping them choose the right architecture while staying aligned with SAP’s clean-core and cloud-first principles.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud：增强版 New User Experience Switch 全面解析
entry.content:
在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中，SAP 持续推进用户体验（UX）的革新，其中 New User Experience Switch（新版用户体验切换功能） 是帮助企业顺利完成应用替换的重要机制。最新版本（2602）对该功能进行了进一步增强，让业务用户与管理员更容易适应新旧应用的切换进程。 

         本文将带你了解这个增强功能的核心设计、工作方式，以及企业在实际使用中可以获得的价值。

一、为什么需要 New User Experience Switch？

         SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 每 6 个月发布一次新版本，其中会逐步引入新应用（phase-in），同时也可能逐步淘汰旧应用（phase-out）。通常，业务用户有 至少 12 个月 的窗口期来适应新的替代应用，但在实际项目中，这段时间可能转瞬即逝。 

         为避免用户错过迁移时机，SAP 引入 New User Experience Switch，通过界面提示与切换选项来“温和推动”用户尝试并过渡到新应用。

二、New User Experience Switch 的三种提示阶段

         切换功能在应用生命周期的不同阶段会呈现不同的提示样式：

1. Information 信息阶段

  • 告诉用户有新的应用版本正在被引入
  • 展示新应用的优势
  • 鼓励用户尝试
  • 用户可以随时切回旧应用

         这是“最温和”的阶段，旨在减少用户对改变的抵触情绪。

2. Warning 警告阶段

         当旧应用已经被标记为 deprecated（即将淘汰） 时，切换功能会显示黄色的警告信息：

  • 告知旧应用将在未来版本中不再提供
  • 提醒用户尽快完成迁移

3. Alert 紧急阶段

         这是旧应用被移除前的最后一个版本，切换功能会以更醒目的方式提示：

  • 强调迁移的紧迫性
  • 显示明确的移除时间点

         在这个阶段，如果用户仍然停留在旧应用上，会面临无法继续使用的风险。

三、切换功能如何呈现？

         SAP 在 2502 版本引入 New User Experience Switch，在 2508 与 2602 的版本中进一步增强：

  • 旧应用启动时出现提示 popover（气泡提示）
  • Shell Bar（顶端工具栏）中出现 切换按钮
  • 用户可在新旧应用之间自由切换（取决于阶段）

         这些交互方式让用户无需离开当前体验即可了解版本状态并快速做出选择。

四、对业务用户意味着什么？

✔ 更轻松的学习曲线

不再需要由管理员单独告知新旧应用替代关系，系统会自动提示。

✔ 提前感知变化

避免新版本发布后突然发现旧应用“消失”，有效减少工作中断。

✔ 自主试用新应用

在 phase-in 阶段，用户可以自由在新旧体验中切换，加速熟悉度提升。

五、对管理员意味着什么？

管理员可以利用此功能：

  • 提前规划应用替换节奏
  • 知道哪些用户仍在使用旧应用
  • 在企业层面推动统一迁移

这让应用治理更加可控，也能减少升级周期带来的沟通成本。

六、为什么说这个功能“让改变更容易”？

New User Experience Switch 正是帮助企业完成改变的机制：

  • 让改变更加可见
  • 让迁移更加自主
  • 让用户更有掌控感

对于每一个正在推进数字化、云化的企业来说，这种设计都能有效降低内部阻力。

结语：更智慧、更温和的用户体验演进机制

         New User Experience Switch 的增强不仅让用户更轻松地迈向 SAP Fiori 的现代化体验，也让管理员和企业能够更从容、可控地管理应用生命周期。

         随着 SAP 持续对 UX 的改进（例如 2602 版本中的 AI 辅助、增强的 Launchpad、手机端优化等），未来企业的 SAP 使用体验将更加智能、一致、顺滑。 

         如果你正在规划 S/4HANA Cloud 的上云旅程或准备迎接新版本，这项功能值得你重点关注。

 

参考文档：SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud – New User Experie... - SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter March 2026
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here a preview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Getting Started with the New SAP Concur Travel for business users

Discover how the new SAP Concur Travel makes planning and booking work trips easier while helping you stay aligned with your company’s travel program. In just a short read, see what’s changing, how it supports your travelers, and how to get enabled along the way.

Full blog

Ready to Upgrade SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?

Don’t let myths hold you back! Many teams hesitate to upgrade SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition due to common misconceptions—like being "too many releases behind" or fearing major disruptions. But what if upgrades could be seamless, strategic, and even empower your business?

Learn more

Meet our experts from SAP Enterprise Support to get updates

Join our session on March 25 to meet our experts from SAP Enterprise Support Academy and SAP Enterprise Support Advisory program. Get latest insights about the move to learning.sap.com and learning highlights and hear which workstream the ESAC innovation program offers in Q1.

Stay tuned and join on You Tube

See how SAP is transforming Ariba into an AI‑native source‑to‑pay platform

SAP is reimagining Ariba as an AI‑native, modern source‑to‑pay platform designed to simplify daily work, improve decision-making, and streamline procurement-to-pay processes. Read the blog to see practical examples, product highlights, and what these changes mean for faster cycle times, stronger cost control, and lower risk across your organization.

Read the full blog

New SAP for Me Services & Support dashboard-now live

We’ve launched a redesigned, user‑centric Services & Support overview tab to make support faster and easier. Enjoy a prominent search, actionable cards for Manage Cases, Quick Access (CIC), service requests and recommendations, unified case management, and simplified navigation — all designed to speed resolutions, boost self‑service, and improve outcomes.

Explore the new dashboard and share your feedback

Heads up for the intelligent front door-one digital experience in SAP for Me

SAP’s new unified entry point in SAP for Me will bring conversational and agentic AI to your fingertips. Discover how effortless navigation, advanced self-service, personalized guidance, and lightning-fast task execution will simplify daily workflows and speed decision-making across your SAP landscape. 

Stay tuned for the launch in May. More details: Intelligent Front Door - Info sheet

Introducing CQC Security Optimization for SAP SuccessFactors-Now in pilot!

Protecting the security and availability of your SAP SuccessFactors environment is vital to keeping your business running smoothly. With the CQC Security Optimization service, you gain proactive analysis and insights to help prevent critical issues before they arise.

Learn more

Your starting point for practical SAP enablement

SAP Enterprise Support Academy is now on learning.sap.com, bringing together hands-on learning, expert guidance, practical resources, and expert-led live sessions that sessions that support you, so you can move from planning to action more quickly.

Learn more here

ALM Summit NA - April 14-16 in Newtown Square-March

The landscape of Application Lifecycle Management is transforming, and you can witness this evolution firsthand at the SAP ALM Summit North America 2026.

Dates: April 14–16, 2026
Location: SAP US Headquarters, Newtown Square, PA

Register here

Customer Success Through Trusted Partner Support Expertise

SAP Cloud customers rely on implementation and operations services from SAP’s partner ecosystem, where an effective support collaboration between partner and SAP is essential for Customer Success.  To unlock incremental value, such as faster issue resolution and risk mitigation, customers are encouraged to select and manage partners also based on support quality.

Read the article and learn more

Related links:

  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy on LSC (learning.sap.com)
  • SAP Support Portal
  • SAP Enterprise Support value maps 
  • Video

entry.title: Embedded Launch Activities: Expanding the Customer Onboarding Journey
entry.content:
Embedded Launch Activities: Expanding the Customer Onboarding Journey

From March 2 onwards, SAP expands Embedded Launch Activities with an enhanced onboarding experience designed to accelerate time to value from contract start and enable implementation teams to engage hands-on with the system at the earliest possible stage.

This expanded framework supports all customers subscribing to any SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition bundle and provides a structured onboarding journey through 1:1 sessions, quarterly 1:m live sessions, and self-service resources. As SAP solutions continue to evolve, so does the Embedded Launch Activities framework — continuously expanding in scope, depth, and relevance.

The Customer Onboarding Journey

1:1 Onboarding Sessions

We engage directly with every customer, always involving implementation partners who bring deep expertise in SAP solutions, industry context, and customer business processes.

These sessions establish a shared understanding across all stakeholders, covering:

  • Project scope and key milestones

  • System architecture and provisioning foundations

  • Commercial entitlements included in the subscription

  • An introduction to artificial intelligence capabilities embedded in the product

  • An overview of SAP Support and engagement models

This early alignment lays the groundwork for a confident and well-structured implementation.

1:1 Entitlement Deep-Dive Sessions

As part of the current commercial bundles, customers have access to additional solutions across treasury, expense reporting, sustainability, and extensibility through SAP Build.

To help implementation teams understand both the value and the implementation path of these solutions, dedicated specialist 1:1 sessions are available, including:

  • SAP Build

  • SAP Taulia

  • SAP Concur

  • SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

  • Sustainability solutions within Supply Chain Premium

These sessions ensure that entitlements translate into practical adoption and measurable business outcomes.

Quality Gates with SAP Cloud ALM

A core element of the expanded framework is guided enablement in setting up quality gates within SAP Cloud ALM.

Through shared-screen sessions, customers are guided to:

  • Define critical checkpoints across the project lifecycle

  • Assess readiness and quality of deliverables

  • Validate compliance with requirements

  • Confirm that key tasks and documentation meet expected standards

Quality gates provide structured decision points that support informed go/no-go decisions and reduce delivery risk.

Alignment Through the Onboarding Plan

The onboarding journey concludes with a dedicated 1:1 session focused on alignment around the Onboarding Plan, ensuring clarity on next steps and ongoing support.

Customers are also encouraged to participate in 1:m live sessions (with recordings available), covering topics such as:

  • Project methodology and implementation best practices

  • End-to-end system architecture

  • SAP product roadmaps

  • Influence SAP and SAP Communities

  • Upgrade cycles and continuous innovation

  • Tools that support sustained value from ERP, including ongoing AI innovations

Guided Enablement and Continuous Learning

As part of the Embedded Launch Activities framework, customers also gain access to Guided Enablement, a structured learning program for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition onboarding.

This program helps implementation teams track progress and build capability throughout the project lifecycle. The learning content is continuously updated to reflect product evolution and emerging best practices.

The Digital Baseline

The Onboarding Resource Center serves as the digital baseline for the onboarding journey. Customers are introduced through a welcome email and subsequently contacted by an Onboarding Advisor to initiate the journey and guide them through each phase.

Embedded Launch Activities provide a consistent, scalable, and evolving onboarding experience — designed to support customers from day one and grow alongside their SAP landscape.

entry.title: How Do I Activate a Situation Type in the Extended Framework?
entry.content:
Situation Handling notifies users about issues requiring their attention. You may choose SAP delivered situation templates or design your own use cases. For the extended framework for Situation Handling, we often discussed how you can create custom situation templates. The framework comes also with SAP delivered use cases that you can easily activate.

This blog post explains how you can activate situation types from SAP delivered situation templates with the extended framework for Situation Handling. It has been available since SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2202 and SAP S/4HANA 2021 FPS02.

 

SAP Delivered Use Cases

Looking for Situation Handling use cases? Visit SAP Help and navigate to Situation Handling, then choose Use Cases. You can narrow your results by filtering for Situation Template Type (either extended object-based or message-based), area, and topic. To learn more about a specific use case, simply check the link in the Situation Template column for a detailed description.

Use Case

For illustration purposes we use the situation template Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Purchasers get a heads-up for purchase contracts that are about to expire.

 

Activation Overview for 2SL and 3SL

Before discussing the activation steps in detail, let me point out the differences between system landscapes and their specific transport paths.

In the extended framework for Situation Handling, you create a situation type from a situation template. Situation templates are only visible in the quality system of a 2-system landscape (2SL) and the development system of a  3-system-landscape (3SL). The situation type needs to be transported to the production system. The transport paths differ between 2SL and 3SL.

Transport the Situation Type in 2SL

In a 2-system-landscape, you transport the situation type from the quality system to the production system.

  • In the quality system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Export the well-defined situation type
  • In the production system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between quality and production system you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 2-System-Landscape

Transport the Situation Type in 3SL

In a 3-system-landscape, transports are required from the development system to both the test and production system. In a 3SL environment you may also have different tenants for development, customizing, and parallel customizing that need to be considered as well.

  • On the customizing tenant in the development system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Export the situation type
  • On the test tenant in the test system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between systems you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Forward the well-defined situation type
  • On the production tenant in the production system you
    • Import the forwarded situation type
    • Enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 3-System-Landscape

Detailed Activation of a Situation Type in 3SL

Now let’s check the detailed steps for a sample use case in a 3-system-landscape as it is more comprehensive. In 2SL the export / import mechanism resembles the one from development system to test system in 3SL.

Depending on your system setting, the transports may also be processed automatically. For optimal clarity, the blog post discusses the transport steps through manual triggers.

Development System

Create a Situation Type from a Situation Template

Log-in as business process configuration expert on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app where you see a list of SAP delivered templates. Use the search option to find the template for our use case Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Select the template and an object page opens. In the header, select Create Situation Type.

Create situation type from situation template

A new page opens with the situation type that contains all information from the situation template. You just need to enter an ID, Name and Description. The type is configured and ready to use. Depending on your requirements you could adapt the settings, for example, by adding filter values for the conditions, changing texts or rescheduling the batch job.

I recommend monitoring the instances to see how situations are handled.

Monitor situations

Let’s check the relevant settings and understand how the configuration works.

There are two Situation Triggers that detect situations and cover different usage scenarios. While the batch-based trigger checks for situations in the background, the event-based trigger reacts on every active change of the contract.

Situation triggers

The situation triggers cover the following cases:

  • The Event-Based Trigger checks the conditions whenever you create or update a purchase contract.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation. The information displayed to the user is defined in Situation Display number 1.
    • Condition 2 is only executed if condition 1 did not match. That is, the validity period is not within the next 30 days, for instance when the contract got extended, and the situation is closed. As nobody will be informed about the closure, the texts are empty (Situation Display number 2).

Event-based trigger

  • In addition to the events, the Batch-Based Trigger regularly checks the validity end date according to the batch schedule.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation.

Batch-based trigger

The configuration for the Batch Job Scheduling specifies that the contract dates are verified every 3 hours throughout the day, beginning at 6:00 a.m.

Batch job scheduling

Export the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Export Software Collection app.

  • On top of the table choose Create Software Collection and a new page opens
  • Choose Add Items and select the situation type you created
  • On the header select Check and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Export and wait until the export is finished successfully

Test System

Import the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the test tenant in the test system. Open the Import Collection app.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Test the Situation Type

As business process specialist you can test the situation type on the corresponding tenant.

Make sure that the situation type is enabled.

Activate the situation type

Then maintain the conditions. The filter values are not transported since they may differ in the various systems and tenants.

  • Select the Event-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Condition 2 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Event-based trigger

  • Select the Batch-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Batch-based trigger

With the situation triggers, you defined when a situation is detected. Now, you need to define who should be informed about situations. Maintain the team(s) in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app and ensure that at least one team is enabled. The filter settings work the same way as with the standard framework for Situation Handling.

Situation recipients

Now you can test the situation type in your business area with the corresponding application data. You can check if there are purchase contracts that match the conditions by choosing Trigger Batch and Manage Instances.

Test the type with trigger batch

If no situations were detected you can log-in as purchaser and create or manipulate a purchasing contract. Add an end date within the next 30 days and the save event will trigger a situation.

When you log-in as a team member of the recipients team you can also check, if you were alerted about the situation.

After the successful test you push the situation type to the production system.

Forward the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator and open the Import Collection app.

  • Select your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Forward and wait until the validation is processed successfully
  • Then choose Forward on the bottom and wait until the export is finished successfully

Production System

Import Collection to Production System

As administrator open the Import Collection app on the production tenant in the production system.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Activate the Situation Type

Log-in as business process specialist and open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app. Like in the test system, you need to maintain the situation type.

  • Enable the situation type
  • Add the filter values for the trigger conditions
  • Ensure that at least one recipient team is enabled

Now you are all set.

Further Information

You find many more blog posts in the Best Practices section of Intelligent Situation Handling in the SAP Community. And you can visit SAP Help Portal for further guidance on Situation Handling.

entry.title: Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 5: Microlearning Videos
entry.content:
Co-Author: Dennis Leslie, Kate Wilson

Are you considering a move to the SAP Central Business Configuration–based SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3‑system landscape – but still unsure what to expect or how to get oriented? Good news: the following microlearning videos are available at no cost to guide you through the essentials.

  • Managing Transports in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video

     

    This video explains how different types of data are transported from the development system to the test and production systems. It outlines a structured and streamlined transport process designed to minimize risk within the 3‑system landscape.

  • Exploring the Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video
    This video highlights the features of the test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The tool simplifies business process testing after release upgrades and is most effective in the 3-system landscape.

  • Navigating Parallel Line in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video
    This video explains the parallel line feature. A parallel line is created as a copy of your main line and provides an additional SAP Central Business Configuration workspace, along with an extra development and test tenant for various use cases. This no‑cost feature is exclusive to the 3‑system landscape.

  • Exploring Developer Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video
    This video highlights the three types of extensibility – key user, developer, and side‑by‑side. While key user and side‑by‑side extensibility are available in both 2‑ and 3‑system landscapes, developer extensibility is exclusive to the 3‑system landscape. Developer extensibility allows you to create customer‑specific extensions to released SAP objects using ABAP, enabling a deeper level of customization.

  • Using the Configuration Localization Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video
    This video explains the features of the Configuration Localization Tool, which enables the creation of customer‑specific countries by localizing business configuration and is available exclusively in the 3‑system landscape.

Explore the power of the 3-system landscape today! To learn more, check out the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3-System Landscape page on SAP Community.

Looking for the earlier posts in this series? You'll find them here:

  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 1: What’s in It for You
  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 2: Making Science’s Success Story 
  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 3: Rapid Conversion Strategies
  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 4: How SAP Supports Your Transition

If you liked this blog, please leave us a thumbs up at the top. Also, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition：为什么越来越多的客户开始使用 Parallel Line（并行线）？
entry.content:
随着企业对业务灵活性、快速创新与高质量上线的要求不断提升，SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition（SAP ERP公有云）在 2025 年正式引入了 Parallel Line（并行线） 的概念。这一能力自发布以来，迅速成为客户最关注的创新之一。截止作者发布此文为止，已有超过200家客户激活并开始使用了并行线功能。

         那么，“并行线”到底是什么？为什么企业迫切需要它？它能解决哪些痛点？希望这篇文章将带你完整理解其价值。

一、为什么需要 Parallel Line？——来自客户的真实声音

         长期以来，SAP ERP公有云的3系统架构（开发、测试、生产）虽然能满足核心实施项目的需要，但在企业实际操作中，经常出现以下诉求：

  • 想要单独的 数据迁移模拟环境
  • 想要并行 SIT/UAT 测试环境
  • 想要 长期稳定培训环境
  • 想要验证 新的第三方集成或新功能
  • 想要为 新增国家、组织结构提供独立配置空间
  • 想让 后续项目不影响当前主线或生产环境

         这些诉求在传统3系统架构中无法完全满足。

         因此，Parallel Line 并行线诞生了。

二、什么是 Parallel Line？

         Parallel Line（以下简称 PL）可以理解为：

“基于主线复制出的第二套配置与测试环境，用于扩展项目、迁移演练和集成测试，并且可选择将配置合并回主线。”

         并行线由以下组成：

  • CBC 内给PL单独的工作区（workspace）
  • 开发系统的并行定制租户（D120）
  • 测试系统的并行测试租户（T120）

         它是一条与主线平行存在的“支线”。

📘 并行线与主线的关系（图示）

三、Parallel Line 并行线能做什么？——典型应用场景

PL 的应用场景可分为两大类：

💡（A）仅需要 Branch（不需合并回主线）

这些场景仅需要复制出并行线，然后单独使用即可，例如：

  • 构建 多轮数据迁移测试
  • 多场景测试：SIT、UAT、回归测试
  • 构建第三方 集成测试环境
  • 提供长期稳定的 培训环境
  • 展示新功能、新的Scope Item等

以下是简化场景矩阵：

💡（B）使用PL后需要合并回主线

这些场景是指在PL并行线中完成相关的操作，然后把所做的更改合并回主线，例如：

  • 添加 新国家/地区
  • 扩展 新的组织架构
  • 增加新的范围项目（Scope item）
  • 增强产品特定配置

 以下是简化场景矩阵：

 

四、使用 Parallel Line并行线 的六大核心好处

 

1. 额外、独立的迁移 / 测试 / 培训环境

避免资源冲突，解决：

  • 测试之间互相覆盖
  • 培训占用测试环境
  • 数据迁移无法反复演练

2. 扩展性配置不影响主线/生产

例如：

  • 新增国家/地区
  • 新公司代码、工厂
  • 新本地化流程
  • 扩展 范围项目（Scope Item）

可以全部在 PL 中进行。

3. 提高数据迁移成功率

因为PL并行线环境可以反复刷新，所以数据迁移可在并行线环境中反复演练，保证上线质量。

4. 更强的集成验证能力

PL 是一套独立并且完整的系统，包含了除生产机之外的整套系统架构，可做：

  • SAP 内部集成验证
  • 第三方接口验证
  • 中间件流程测试

5. 稳定培训环境

         也有不少客户需要给新员工提供一个培训环境，PL并行线不影响生产、不被当前测试影响、同时也会跟随版本升级而升级，是一个作为培训的最佳环境。

**6. 最重要：**无需额外订阅费用

PL并行线环境包含在了客户3系统云订阅中，无需额外付费。客户可在SAP for Me里自助激活。

激活流程大致如下：

1. 在SAP for Me里，选择Service & Support，点击New Service Request：

 2. 选择Partner & Other Cloud Services，选择Create Service Request：

 3. 在Service Catalog中输入关键字Parallel，会找到并行线相关服务。Check Prerequisites服务用来检查是否有创建并行线的前提条件，Branch服务用来创建并行线。请首先选择Check Prerequisites服务，通过之后选择Branch服务：

 

五、并行线 vs 沙箱：业务场景差异

         在与一些客户的交流中，经常有一些客户会问到并行线系统与沙箱（sandbox）或者starter系统的区别，现把两者的区别整理如下：

 

一句话总结：

沙箱用于“看”，并行线用于“干”。

六、为什么并行线值得企业采用？

从业务管理层角度看，并行线的最大价值在于：

  • ✔ 提高上线成功率
  • ✔ 提供快速扩展能力
  • ✔ 降低创新风险
  • ✔ 分离配置波次，保证稳定性
  • ✔ 支撑全球化、多国家 rollout

来自使用过并行线功能的真实客户评价：

“我们真诚地认为，并行线环境已经，并且将继续对 SAP 项目的成功产生重大影响。”

“我们将并行线视为非常重要的创新，对未来新的实施至关重要。如果我们没有并行线，生产租户将面临风险。”

七、结语

         Parallel Line 并行线已经成为 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中最具战略价值的能力之一。无论是数据迁移、测试、培训，还是复杂的扩展配置，它都带来了前所未有的灵活性与安全性。

         如果你的企业正在：

  • 推全球化 rollout
  • 多批次上线
  • 多系统集成
  • 有复杂迁移场景

         并行线几乎是必备能力。

         如果您看了这篇文章，想了解更多关于Parallel Line并行线的内容，我也把几个非常有用的文档链接分享如下：

  • Parallel Line Overview文档
  • 并行线SAP Help Portal链接
  • Parallel Line FAQ

         当然，有任何问题也可以与我联系。感谢阅读！

entry.title: Enhancing Cost Center Security with Company Code Restrictions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Security and access control are fundamental pillars of enterprise resource planning systems. As organizations grow and their data becomes increasingly sensitive, the need for granular authorization controls becomes paramount. SAP S/4HANA Cloud continues to evolve its authorization framework to meet these critical business requirements. For example: Authorization on company code level for app Manage Cost Centers F1443A

One significant enhancement to the authorization architecture is the introduction of Company Code restrictions based on company code. This new feature represents an important step forward in helping organizations maintain tighter control over who can access and manage cost center information across different business entities.

In this blog post, we explore this authorization enhancement, its implications for system administrators, and the steps necessary to ensure a smooth transition when this feature becomes active in upcoming versions(2608) of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Scenario: Understanding the Business Need

The Challenge of Cost Center Access Control

Cost centers are critical organizational structures used for allocating costs, tracking expenses, and managing financial reporting across different departments and business units. In large organizations with multiple company codes (representing different legal entities or business divisions), the challenge of managing who can access which cost centers becomes increasingly complex.

Consider this real-world scenario:

A global manufacturing company operates multiple subsidiaries, each with its own company code:

- Company Code 1000 (Europe Division)

- Company Code 2000 (Asia Division)

- Company Code 3000 (Americas Division)

Each division has its own cost centers and financial managers. Previously, without granular Company Code restrictions, a user authorized for cost center management might inadvertently access cost center data across all company codes—potentially exposing sensitive financial information and creating compliance risks.

The Solution: Company Code Restrictions

The new Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code feature addresses this security gap by enabling administrators to:

  1. Limit Cost Center Visibility: Restrict users to view only cost centers associated with their authorized company codes
  2. Control Modification Rights: Ensure that users can only modify cost center master data within their assigned company codes
  3. Enforce Compliance: Meet regulatory requirements for data segregation and access controls
  4. Improve Audit Trails: Create clear, auditable records of who accessed which cost centers

Implementation Details

 1.Authorization Requirements

To configure and manage Company Code restrictions, administrators must have specific authorization:

Required Business Catalog:

- `SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM` – This business catalog must be assigned to administrators responsible for identity and access management (IAM)

This ensures that only qualified IAM professionals can modify restriction configurations and prevent unauthorized changes to security settings.

 

2.Configuration Steps

Administrators must take proactive steps to configure the Company Code restriction before it becomes mandatory. Here's how:

Key Configuration Tool:

- App: Maintain Business Roles (F1492)

- Action: Preconfigure Company Code restriction values for all business roles that manage cost center master data

Configuration Process:

  1. Open the Maintain Business Roles app (F1492)
  2. Select the business role requiring Company Code restrictions
  3. Navigate to the restriction configuration section
  4. Assign appropriate company code values to the restriction
  5. Save and activate the configuration

Best Practice: Conduct this configuration during a low-usage period and test thoroughly in a sandbox environment before applying to production.

 

3. Affected Technical Objects

The Company Code restriction will impact access control across multiple layers of the SAP system:

CDS Views (Core Data Services):

- `I_CostCenter` – Main cost center master data view

- `I_CostCenterVH` – Cost center value help view

- `I_CostCenterSTDVH` – Cost center standard value help

- `I_CostCenterTP_2` – Cost center transactional posting view

Data Dictionary Value Help:

- `KOST` – Traditional value help for cost centers

Business Object Interface:
